resources.string: Step 1: Select Compose Multiplatform Project Root
Release build - required for App Store submission
Debug build - for local testing only, not for submission.
Required for Mac App Store submission
For direct distribution outside the App Store — not valid for this flow.
A separate certificate is required for signing the installer package (.pkg), distinct from the Application identity above.

These values cannot be fetched programmatically because they are the credentials used to authenticate with Apple. \n\nGo to the link below and select the "Team Keys" tab (recommended as they work across your whole account). Look for the field labelled "Issuer ID" near the top - it's a UUID like 48e54131-..., and there's a "Copy" button next to it.

Check the "Active" keys table under the "Team Keys" tab first. If you already have a key from a previous project (with Admin or App Manager access), you can reuse its 10-character "Key ID" here. \n\nIf you have no active keys: click the "+" next to "Active", give it a name, choose "Admin" or "App Manager" access, then download the .p8 file immediately (Apple only allows one download ever).

The .p8 file you downloaded when the key was created. ComposeShip will try to find this automatically. If it doesn't, common locations are ~/private_keys/ or wherever you saved it - search your system for files ending in .p8 if unsure. If you\'ve lost it, you will need to revoke the old key and create a new one.
Open App Store Connect API Page
Your essential suite of developer utility tools for Compose Multiplatform. Streamlining your workflow across Android, iOS, Web, and Desktop.
🚀 macOS Release Tool: Effortless App Store submission.
🛠 More tools coming soon: Image resizing, background removal, and more.
App Store Connect credentials are required
API Key file not found at %s
Not a Gradle project (missing settings/build files)
